questions.question: What is the property of an object to resist changes in its motion?
questions.explanation: The tendency of a body to continue in its state of rest or uniform motion unless acted upon by an external force.

questions.question: What is the unit of measurement for force?
questions.explanation: The newton (N) is the SI unit of force, defined as the force that accelerates a 1-kilogram mass by 1 meter per second squared.

questions.question: What is the relationship between force and acceleration?
questions.explanation: The force (F) applied to an object is equal to its mass (m) times its acceleration (a).

questions.question: What is the effect of a magnet on copper?
questions.explanation: When a magnet moves near copper, it generates tiny electric currents called eddy currents, which create their own magnetic field.

notes.title: Inertia and Mass Relationship
notes.content: The greater the mass of an object, the greater its inertia. Inertia is directly proportional to mass.

notes.title: Force and Acceleration Formula
notes.content: F=ma (force equals mass times acceleration). This formula describes the relationship between force and acceleration.

notes.title: Eddy Currents in Copper
notes.content: When a magnet moves near copper, it generates tiny electric currents called eddy currents. These currents create their own magnetic field, which opposes the magnet's motion.

notes.title: Unit of Measurement for Force
notes.content: The newton (N) is the SI unit of force, defined as the force that accelerates a 1-kilogram mass by 1 meter per second squared.

notes.title: Inertia and Motion Relationship
notes.content: The tendency of a body to continue in its state of rest or uniform motion unless acted upon by an external force is called inertia.

notes.title: Force and Acceleration Relationship
notes.content: The force (F) applied to an object is equal to its mass (m) times its acceleration (a).

facts.fact: The newton (N) is the SI unit of force.
Inertia is directly proportional to mass.
Eddy currents are generated when a magnet moves near copper, creating their own magnetic field.
The force (F) applied to an object is equal to its mass (m) times its acceleration (a).
Inertia is the tendency of a body to continue in its state of rest or uniform motion unless acted upon by an external force.
Eddy currents create their own magnetic field, which opposes the magnet's motion.
The newton (N) is defined as the force that accelerates a 1-kilogram mass by 1 meter per second squared.
Inertia is directly proportional to mass, so objects with greater mass have greater inertia.
When a magnet moves near copper, it generates tiny electric currents called eddy currents.
